faq.title: Frequently asked questions about pricing
faq.q1: Why a flat fee rather than a commission?
faq.a1: Our flat fee helps you plan your costs every month, regardless of your employee's salary. Unlike commission-based providers, you do not pay more when you increase your employee's salary or hours.
faq.a2: No commitment at all. With the monthly plan at CHF 12.99, you can cancel anytime. The annual plan at CHF 99 saves you money and works out to about CHF 8.25 per month.
faq.a3: Absolutely none. The price displayed is the price you pay. No commission, no surcharge.
faq.a4: Everything: personalised employer checklist for your canton, pay slip generation, employment contract template, social contribution calculations, step-by-step administrative guidance and email support.

step1.title: Create your account

step3.detail: Download pay slips, contracts and annual statements at any time. You receive a notification as soon as a new document is available.

about.title: About HappySalary
about.subtitle: A Swiss company serving household employers.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: We believe that every household employee deserves a declared salary and that every employer deserves a simple, affordable solution to meet their legal obligations. HappySalary was founded to make household employment in Switzerland accessible, transparent and fully compliant.

quality.title: Quality
quality.description: We deliver a high-quality service, designed by specialists in Swiss payroll and employment law.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.description: Our pricing is clear and fixed. No hidden commissions, no surprises on your invoice.

security.title: Security
security.description: Your data is hosted in Switzerland and protected to the highest standards.

simplicity.title: Simplicity
simplicity.description: An intuitive interface and a streamlined process, because managing an employee should not be complicated.

story.title: Our Story
story.description: HappySalary was born from a simple observation: in Switzerland, declaring a household employee is too complicated and too expensive. We built a platform that provides tools and guides for the key administrative tasks — contribution calculations, LAA guidance, contract templates, pay slips — for a fixed, transparent price. Our goal: making legal household employment accessible to every Swiss household.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find answers to the most common questions about HappySalary.

general.a1: HappySalary is a Swiss platform for household employers. It calculates salaries and social contributions, generates pay slips and contract templates, and guides you through LAA and registration procedures — all for a fixed monthly fee.
general.a2: Anyone in Switzerland who employs household staff: cleaning lady, nanny, au pair, elderly care assistant, gardener or any other domestic employee.
general.q3: In which cantons is HappySalary available?
general.a3: HappySalary is available in all Swiss cantons. We apply the specific tariffs and regulations for each canton, particularly with regard to withholding tax.
general.q4: How do I create my account?
general.a4: Visit our website, click on ‘Get Started’ and follow the registration steps. You will need your employee's personal details (name, address, AHV number) and the agreed salary.

pricingFaq.q1: Why a flat fee instead of a commission?
pricingFaq.a1: A flat fee helps you keep costs predictable and fair. With a commission model, the better you pay your employee, the more the service costs you. At HappySalary, the price stays the same regardless of the salary amount.
pricingFaq.q2: Are social contributions included in the flat fee?
pricingFaq.a2: The flat fee covers the calculation and generation of social contribution documents. The contributions themselves (AHV/IV/EO/ALV) are paid by you directly to the compensation fund, with no mark-up whatsoever on our part.
pricingFaq.a3: No, there is no minimum commitment. You can cancel your subscription at any time. The service ends at the end of the current month.

servicesFaq.q1: What types of employment contracts do you offer?
servicesFaq.a1: We generate employment contracts that comply with the Swiss Code of Obligations and the applicable cantonal standard employment contracts for domestic workers. Each contract is individually tailored to your situation.
servicesFaq.q2: How do the pay slips work?
servicesFaq.a2: Each month, we calculate your employee's net salary after deducting social contributions and, where applicable, withholding tax. The pay slip is available as a PDF in your account dashboard.
servicesFaq.a3: HappySalary calculates the LAA (UVG) contribution and integrates it into your pay slips. You remain responsible for affiliating with an accident insurer — our checklist guides you through the process step by step.

legalFaq.q1: Am I required to declare my household employee?
legalFaq.a1: Yes, in Switzerland every employer is legally required to register their household employee with the compensation office (AHV) and to take out accident insurance (UVG). Undeclared work is a criminal offence.
legalFaq.a2: Foreign employees without a C settlement permit are subject to withholding tax. HappySalary automatically calculates the amount based on the tariff of the employee's canton of residence and deducts it from the gross salary.
legalFaq.q3: What are my obligations as an employer?
legalFaq.a3: As a household employer, you must register your employee with social insurance, pay employer contributions, take out accident insurance, draw up an employment contract and issue monthly pay slips. HappySalary guides you and provides the tools to meet these obligations.

marie.name: Marie L.
marie.text: HappySalary has taken a huge weight off my shoulders. I was paying a 5% commission on my cleaner's salary with another provider. Now I pay a flat fee and everything is included. Simple and transparent.

thomas.name: Thomas M.
thomas.text: We have a full-time nanny and the paperwork was a nightmare. With HappySalary, I generated the contract and payslips in a few clicks. The guide really helped me with the contributions.

anna.name: Anna S.

peter.name: Peter W.
peter.text: My mother needs a home carer and we wanted to do everything by the book. The checklist helped us follow every procedure without stress. The flat fee is unbeatable.

sophie.name: Sophie B.
sophie.text: My gardener only comes 4 hours a month, but I still had to register him. HappySalary guided me step by step and I generate the payslip in 2 minutes. Simple and affordable.

marco.name: Marco R.
marco.text: The email support is excellent. I had a question about EO contributions and received a clear, detailed answer within an hour. The employer guide is truly comprehensive.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.a1: Yes, the net salary calculation is completely free. The full breakdown of deductions and pay slip generation are reserved for subscribers.
faq.q2: Are the rates up to date?
faq.a2: Yes, we update AHV, ALV and UVG rates every year according to federal and cantonal guidelines.
faq.q3: Do I really need to register my household employee?
faq.a3: Yes, in Switzerland, every employer of household staff is required to register their employee with social insurance, even for just a few hours per month.
faq.a4: HappySalary costs CHF 12.99/month or CHF 99/year. Everything is included: salary calculation, contribution documents, LAA guidance and contract templates.

contracts.title: Employment contract template
contracts.taskDescriptionPlaceholder: E.g. Cleaning, ironing, grocery shopping, childcare...
contracts.specialConditionsPlaceholder: E.g. Equipment provided, pets, keys, alarm...
contracts.savedToDocuments: The contract has been saved to your documents.

tooltips.trial: The trial period allows both parties to terminate the contract with 7 days' notice. Legal maximum: 3 months.
tooltips.activityRate: Percentage of working time compared to full-time. 100% = full-time.
tooltips.vacation: If checked, a surcharge of 8.33% (4 weeks) or 10.64% (5 weeks) is added to the hourly rate to compensate for holidays.
tooltips.insurance: Accident insurance (UVG) is mandatory from 8 hours of work per week with the same employer.
tooltips.notice: Swiss statutory notice periods: 7 days during trial, then 1 month (1st year), 2 months (2-9 years), 3 months (from 10 years).
tooltips.thirteenth: The 13th salary typically corresponds to 8.33% of the annual gross salary, paid at year-end or distributed monthly.
tooltips.paymentMethod: How the salary is paid to your employee.
tooltips.simplifiedProcedure: Art. 37a OASI Act — simplified procedure with 5% flat tax. Applicable if salary ≤ CHF 22,050/year.

errors.endDateRequired: End date required for fixed-term contracts.
errors.minMaxInvalid: Minimum must be less than maximum.

contracts.disclaimer: This document is provided as a template and must be adapted to your situation. HappySalary does not guarantee its legal compliance. The employer remains responsible for verifying the applicable legal provisions.
contracts.upgradeMessage: Upgrade to the Pro plan to access customisable employment contract templates.
contracts.noContractsDescription: Generate a customised employment contract template for your employees.

pdf.name: Name
pdf.salaryClause: The salary is paid monthly. Statutory social contributions (AHV/IV/EO/ALV) are deducted in accordance with the rates in force.
pdf.vacationIncludedClause: The salary includes a holiday compensation surcharge of 8.33% in accordance with art. 329d CO.
pdf.vacationClause: The employee is entitled to 4 weeks of paid holiday per year of service.
pdf.vacationClause5: The employee is entitled to 5 weeks of paid holiday per year of service.
pdf.vacationIncludedClause5: The salary includes a holiday compensation supplement of 10.64% in accordance with Art. 329d CO.
pdf.insuranceYes: The employer undertakes to provide accident insurance (UVG) in compliance with statutory requirements for the employee.
pdf.insuranceNo: As the employee works less than 8 hours per week, accident insurance is not provided by the employer. The employee must ensure they are covered by their own health insurance.
pdf.noticeClauseCDI: During the trial period, the notice period is 7 days. After the trial period: 1 month during the 1st year, 2 months from the 2nd to the 9th year, 3 months from the 10th year onwards.
pdf.noticeClauseCDD: The contract ends automatically on the agreed date. Any early termination must be by mutual agreement.
pdf.noticeClauseCustom: The notice period is set at {period} for the end of a month, after the trial period.
pdf.trialClause: A trial period of {duration} is agreed from the start date of the contract. During this period, either party may terminate the contract with 7 days' notice.
pdf.accommodationClause: The employer provides accommodation for the employee. The terms (rent, utilities, inventory) are defined separately.
pdf.mealsClause: The employer provides meals to the employee during working hours. The value is determined according to tax authority guidelines.
pdf.legalDisclaimer: This contract is established in accordance with the provisions of the Swiss Code of Obligations (Art. 319 et seq. CO). It constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding the terms of employment. Any amendment must be made in writing and signed by both parties.
pdf.salaryNetClause: The net salary will be determined after deduction of mandatory social insurance contributions (OASI/DI/APG, UI, AIA).
pdf.simplifiedTaxNote: Under the simplified accounting procedure (Art. 37a OASI Act), a source tax of 5% is levied on the gross salary.
pdf.thirteenthSalaryClause: The employee is entitled to a 13th salary of CHF {amount}, paid proportionally at the end of the calendar year.

payslip.subtitle: Enter the hours worked and we'll guide you step by step.
payslip.laaRatesHint: The exact rate depends on your insurer. Leave empty to use estimated default rates.
payslip.laaEstimationNote: LAA rates marked with * are estimates. The exact rate depends on your insurer. You can enter your actual rates in the advanced settings.
payslip.enterHours: Enter the hours worked to see the summary.
payslip.subscriptionMessage: Subscribe to a plan to access salary calculation and pay slip generation.
payslip.savedToDocuments: The pay slip has been saved to your documents.
payslip.alreadyGenerated: A pay slip for this period already exists.
payslip.disclaimer: This pay slip is based on the information provided. The rates applied are those in force. The employer remains responsible for verifying the amounts.

guide.title: Next steps
guide.step2desc: Social charges to reserve for the annual declaration.
guide.step3desc: Provide a copy to your employee.
guide.step4desc: At year end, declare salaries to your OASI fund.
guide.pdfStep2: Set aside CHF {amount} for social charges.
guide.pdfStep3: Provide a copy of this pay slip to your employee.
guide.pdfStep4: Keep this document for your annual declaration.

documentsPage.title: Documents
documentsPage.noDocumentsDesc: Your payslips and employment contracts will appear here once generated.

checklistPage.title: Employer steps
checklistPage.subtitle: Follow the steps to correctly employ your household staff.
checklistPage.noActiveEmployees: No active employees. Add an employee to see your checklist.

contractsList.title: Employment Contracts
contractsList.noContractsDesc: Generate a Swiss-compliant employment contract for your employee.

tooltips.hoursWorked: Actual hours worked this month. Adjust if different from the contract (absences, overtime).
tooltips.hourlyRate: Gross hourly wage as agreed in the employment contract. If you have a monthly salary, divide by the monthly hours.
tooltips.canton: Employee's work canton. Determines family allowances and cantonal contribution rates.
tooltips.salaryType: Hourly = calculated on hours worked. Monthly = fixed amount. Must match the employment contract.
tooltips.vacationHandling: 'Added' = 8.33% (4 wks) or 10.64% (5 wks) supplement added monthly. 'Included' = hourly rate already includes vacation pay.
tooltips.simplifiedProcedure: Art. 37a OASI Act — simplified procedure with 5% flat tax. Applicable if salary ≤ CHF 22,050/year per employer.
tooltips.nbuOption: Non-occupational accident insurance (NBU). Mandatory if ≥ 8h/week. 'Auto' enables deduction based on declared hours.
